steps.title: Recognize the central angle
steps.content: Angle $\angle ROS$ has its vertex at $O$ (the center) with rays extending to points $R$ and $S$ on the circle. This is a central angle, and it directly intercepts arc $RS$.

steps.title: Use the central angle property
steps.content: The measure of a central angle equals the measure of its intercepted arc. Therefore, $\angle ROS = 100^{\circ}$.
